categories.category: Bidding and Estimating Software with AI

tools.name: Togal.AI
tools.description: AI-powered floor plan takeoff and estimating software that detects, measures, compares, and labels spaces directly from architectural plans. Claims 98% accuracy on floor plan takeoffs and completes a full architectural takeoff in 12 minutes.
tools.pricing: Essential: $199/user/month; Growth: $299/user/month ($17,940/year for 5-user team at Growth tier). 7-day free trial available.
tools.built_for: GC and sub (GC, painting, flooring, drywall, home builders)
tools.results: Full architectural takeoff completed in 12 minutes; 98% claimed accuracy
tools.integrations: Integrates with major construction platforms; includes document search (TogalCHAT)

tools.name: Beam AI
tools.description: Trade-specific automated takeoffs for plumbing, electrical, concrete, roofing, flooring, and HVAC. Allows subcontractors and suppliers to scale bid capacity without adding overhead staff.
tools.results: Enables scaling of bid capacity without increasing overhead
tools.integrations: Platform integrations available on request

tools.name: STACK
tools.description: Digital takeoff and estimating platform with AI-assisted plan measurement and quantity extraction. Particularly strong for MEP trades and targets trade contractors bidding high volumes of work weekly.
tools.pricing: Multiple tiers from free trial to enterprise
tools.results: Documented speed improvements on high-volume bidding; used widely across MEP trades
tools.integrations: Integrates with major estimating and project management platforms

tools.name: BidIntell
tools.description: SaaS pre-bid platform that uses AI to analyze incoming bid requests and generate a BidIndex score (0-100 scale) to help estimators determine which project invitations are worth pursuing.
tools.results: Streamlines go/no-go decision-making; reduces wasted bid effort
tools.integrations: Standalone platform with API connectivity

tools.name: Downtobid / ConstructionBids.AI
tools.description: AI-powered bid management that automatically identifies scopes of work, finds qualified local commercial subcontractors, sends personalized bid invites, and analyzes bids for completeness and competitiveness.
tools.built_for: Both (GC-primary for invitation management; sub for bid response)
tools.results: 40% time savings on bid preparation; 30% improvement in win rates documented
tools.integrations: Integrates with Procore and other construction management platforms

categories.category: Submittal and RFI Tracking and Automation

tools.name: Trunk Tools
tools.description: AI platform for construction document search using natural language to instantly find information across drawings, specs, and project archives. Generates AI-assisted submittals with automated tracking and daily reports with photos.
tools.results: Users report time savings equivalent to months of full-time work per project year
tools.integrations: Integrates with Procore and major document management platforms

tools.name: Ichi
tools.description: AI-powered submittal review tool that cross-references submitted materials against project specifications and building codes, catching compliance issues early. Reduces review cycles significantly.
tools.results: Reduces submittal review time from 45-90 minutes down to 10-15 minutes per submittal
tools.integrations: Integrates with major construction PM platforms

tools.name: Bluebeam
tools.description: Industry-standard construction document platform handling RFI and submittal workflows with AI-enhanced digital markup, review tools, and collaboration features.
tools.results: Widely adopted across the industry with documented workflow efficiency gains
tools.integrations: Direct integration with Procore and Autodesk; 500+ partner integrations

tools.name: Civils.ai
tools.description: AI-powered natural language search and analysis across entire project archives, including drawings, specs, and correspondence. Enables teams to find information instantly without manual searching.
tools.results: Users report time savings equivalent to months of full-time work over a project year
tools.integrations: Connects to common document storage and project management platforms

tools.name: eSUB
tools.description: Mobile-first construction management platform built exclusively for commercial subcontractors. Manages daily reporting, change orders, submittals, RFIs, time cards, and purchase orders. Connects field to office.
tools.pricing: Starting at $12/user/month — one of the most affordable options for subcontractors
tools.results: 4.7/5 user rating; proven adoption across MEP, concrete, drywall, and other specialty trades
tools.integrations: Integrates with QuickBooks, Foundation, Sage 100, and Procore

tools.name: BuildOps
tools.description: AI-native operating system for commercial service contractors. Unifies scheduling, dispatching, job tracking, invoicing, and customer management. AI auto-generates invoice summaries from technician notes, recommends best technician for each call, and converts POs to invoice line items.
tools.results: Documented reduction in administrative processing time and billing errors
tools.integrations: Integrates with accounting platforms and major construction PM tools

tools.name: CompanyCam
tools.description: AI-powered photo documentation and field reporting tool. AI generates daily reports without manual typing by combining job site photos with automated summaries. Supports voice notes and time-stamped photo logs.
tools.results: Eliminates manual daily report writing; documented time savings on field documentation
tools.integrations: Integrates with Procore, JobNimbus, and ServiceTitan

tools.name: Rhumbix
tools.description: Mobile application providing a single source of truth for field-generated data. T&M tags can be automated, allowing foremen to document time and materials from the field using photos, attachments, and pricing. When data flows from jobsite to invoice automatically, admin tasks drop 80%.
tools.results: 80% reduction in administrative tasks when fully automated; integrates directly with billing workflows
tools.integrations: Integrates with Procore, Viewpoint, and other major construction platforms

tools.name: SmartBarrel
tools.description: Biometric time tracking using facial recognition and GPS geofencing. Creates indisputable proof for every hour billed, critical for T&M work. Tracks labor costs in real time with field-verified data.
tools.pricing: Custom quote-based pricing; Basic, Pro, and Enterprise tiers available
tools.built_for: Both (subcontractors and GCs for labor cost tracking)
tools.results: Eliminates time fraud; provides verified T&M documentation for billing disputes
tools.integrations: Integrates with major payroll and construction management systems

tools.name: Foundation Software T&M Billing
tools.description: Dedicated Time and Material Billing module within Foundation's construction accounting platform. Handles accurate construction invoicing, tracks billable labor and materials, and integrates with job cost accounting.
tools.pricing: Available on request; part of Foundation accounting suite
tools.built_for: Both (specialty subs and GCs with T&M contract work)
tools.results: Documented reduction in billing errors and unbilled work
tools.integrations: Integrates with eSUB, Procore, and major accounting systems

tools.name: Document Crunch
tools.company: Document Crunch (being acquired by Trimble, announced April 2026)
tools.description: AI contract intelligence platform that uses proprietary AI and machine learning to simplify construction contracts. Identifies critical risk provisions, provides project playbooks translating legal jargon into plain English, and reduces contract review time from days to minutes.
tools.results: Reduces contract review from days to minutes; Trimble acquisition in April 2026 validates market position
tools.integrations: Native integration with Procore marketplace and Microsoft Word

tools.name: Pelles AI
tools.description: Generative AI platform for construction that generates proposals, RFIs, and submittal logs in your company's format. Allows teams to handle more document volume with greater consistency and confidence.
tools.results: Enables teams to process significantly more documents with fewer staff
tools.integrations: Connects to common construction document management platforms

tools.name: Procore with Helix AI
tools.description: Dominant construction management platform now featuring AI-powered change order management, Agent Builder for workflow automation, automated compliance checking, and Procore Assist. Scheduling reached general availability in Q1 2026 with submittals integrated.
tools.pricing: $499-$1,099+/month depending on modules and annual construction volume
tools.results: 38% market share among GCs with $25M+ annual revenue; documented productivity gains across workflows
tools.integrations: 500+ integrations including Sage, Vista, Autodesk, CMiC, Workday, Viewpoint

tools.name: ContraVault AI
tools.description: AI platform built specifically for the pre-award workflow. Helps teams move from opportunity qualification to submission-ready proposals with minimal manual effort. Automates scope gap analysis and compliance checking against bid requirements.
tools.results: Documented reduction in proposal preparation time; improves bid submission consistency
tools.integrations: Connects with major bid management and construction platforms

tools.name: BidIntell
tools.description: Pre-bid analysis SaaS platform that uses AI to score incoming bid invitations on a 100-point BidIndex, enabling data-driven go/no-go decisions. Analyzes project details, owner history, and competitive landscape.
tools.results: Reduces wasted estimating effort on low-probability bids; improves overall bid win rate
tools.integrations: Standalone platform with API connectivity to bid management tools

tools.name: AutoRFP.ai
tools.description: Automates proposal generation and bid management, ensuring consistency and compliance with project requirements. Uses AI to generate proposal content, track bid responses, and manage the full bid lifecycle.
tools.results: Significant time savings on proposal writing and formatting
tools.integrations: Integrates with major construction and document management platforms

tools.name: ALICE Technologies
tools.description: AI simulation-based construction scheduling for projects $50M+. Runs thousands of schedule simulations to optimize sequencing and resource allocation. Particularly strong for GCs managing complex infrastructure, industrial, and commercial projects.
tools.pricing: Enterprise pricing for projects $50M+; custom quotes
tools.built_for: GC (general contractors and project owners on large projects)
tools.results: 17% avg reduction in project duration; 14% labor cost savings; 12% equipment cost savings. GC saved $25M+ on interstate highway project; Suffolk Construction recovered 42 days on life sciences project; data center team recovered 63 days; hyperscale data center team protected $32M in revenue
tools.integrations: Integrates with Primavera P6, Microsoft Project, and major scheduling platforms

tools.name: Linarc
tools.description: Cloud-based construction management with AI-powered scheduling featuring delay prediction and schedule simulation. Includes subcontractor portals, real-time dashboards, task management, submittals, RFIs, change orders, daily reports, and timecards.
tools.built_for: Both (GCs, subcontractors, and owners in commercial construction)
tools.results: Cuts RFI response time by 30%; predicts critical path delays before they materialize
tools.integrations: Open API connects to ERP and accounting systems

tools.name: nPlan
tools.description: AI that uses machine learning to analyze historical project data and forecast project risks, enabling construction teams to de-risk schedules before problems materialize. Provides probabilistic schedule analysis.
tools.pricing: Enterprise pricing for large infrastructure and commercial projects
tools.built_for: GC (large GCs and owners on major infrastructure projects)
tools.results: Documented risk identification months before traditional methods; used on major infrastructure programs
tools.integrations: Integrates with Primavera P6 and major scheduling platforms

tools.name: Oracle Construction and Engineering Advisor for Safety
tools.description: AI-enabled predictive intelligence solution made generally available in early 2026. Allows construction firms to forecast project safety incidents and support safer jobsites using predictive analytics across project data.
tools.pricing: Enterprise pricing as part of Oracle Construction and Engineering suite
tools.results: Predictive safety incident forecasting; part of Oracle's broader construction AI push
tools.integrations: Integrates within Oracle Construction and Engineering ecosystem

tools.name: Syntora
tools.description: AI-powered safety monitoring for small construction firms using computer vision to automatically detect on-site hazards and PPE violations from video feeds. Provides continuous oversight and can review 8 hours of site footage in under 15 minutes.
tools.pricing: Custom pricing for small to mid-size contractors
tools.built_for: Both (small to mid-size GCs and subcontractors)
tools.results: Continuous PPE and hazard monitoring; 8 hours of footage reviewed in under 15 minutes
tools.integrations: Works with standard jobsite camera systems

tools.name: SafetyEvolution
tools.description: Construction EHS software platform with AI-driven compliance monitoring. Provides automated certification tracking, safety analytics, incident management, and regulatory compliance documentation.
tools.results: Replaces 1-2 hours of daily manual compliance checks with automated monitoring; reduces admin burden by approximately 80%
tools.integrations: Integrates with Procore and major HR/payroll systems

tools.name: Sysgenpro AI Safety Compliance
tools.description: AI-driven safety compliance automation providing continuous certification tracking, automated alerts, and safety analytics. Moves safety management from reactive to proactive through AI monitoring.
tools.results: Documented safety incident reductions of 40-50%; eliminates manual daily compliance checks
tools.integrations: API-based integration with construction PM platforms

market_context.owner_expectations: By 2026, owners and large GCs expect specialty subs to operate with full digital transparency. Key requirements include: RFI and submittal management through digital platforms (Procore, Autodesk ACC) rather than email; real-time daily reports with photo documentation uploaded to GC systems; digital timecard and T&M documentation with GPS verification; participation in digital prequalification processes with current insurance, safety, and financial data; and full participation in digital bid, change order, and contract document ecosystems. Procore holds ~38% market share among GCs with $25M+ annual revenue — subs must operate within these platforms. 36% of GCs now prioritize AI contract analytics and risk management and are beginning to expect the same from subs. 61% of contractors use or plan to use AI bidding tools by mid-2026 (up from 34% in 2024).
market_context.adoption_cost: For a specialty sub with 5-20 PMs: Lean stack (estimating + field management + photo reporting): $6,000-$21,000/year. Mid-tier stack adding prebid analysis and change order AI: $15,000-$55,000/year. Full enterprise stack: $36,000-$72,000/year for 5-user deployment per industry averages; $60,000-$120,000/year for 20-user full-stack deployment including implementation and training. Individual tool costs: eSUB at $12/user/month ($720-$2,880/year for 5-20 users); CompanyCam at $36/user/month ($2,160-$8,640/year); Togal.AI at $199-$299/user/month ($11,940-$71,760/year for 5-20 users); BuildOps at $299/user/month ($17,940-$71,760/year).
market_context.roi: Documented ROI from AI adoption: 22-31% win rate improvement from AI-powered bid matching (tracked across 1,200+ contractors); 60% reduction in bid preparation time with AI estimating tools; 15% material waste reduction saving ~$25,000 per project for one specialty sub; 24 labor hours saved per project on documentation; 8-14% material cost reductions from AI procurement ($160,000-$280,000/year on $2M material spend); 80% reduction in administrative tasks when invoicing and documentation fully automated; 15-20% closer to original project timelines for firms using digital scheduling; 40-50% safety incident reductions with AI monitoring. 38% of contractors now report measurable business impact from AI (vs. 17% one year ago). Most targeted applications pay for themselves within 6-18 months; full-stack ROI typically within 2-3 years.
